name: Coordinated Civil Unrest
description: Simultaneous coordinated aggressive protests paralyze Toronto, Ottawa, and Vancouver. Starts as marches, escalates to riots. Police overwhelmed in all three cities. Citizen radio operators begin noting the suspicious simultaneity.

protest_toronto.summary: Coordinated aggressive protest at Toronto City Hall escalating to full riot.
protest_toronto.main_event: Riot confirmed at Nathan Phillips Square. Police lines broken. Financial district threatened.
protest_toronto.prediction: Protest will spread to Queen's Park and financial district within 20 minutes.
protest_toronto.suggestion: Request mutual aid from Peel and York Regional Police. Close Queen St corridor.

protest_ottawa.summary: Violent protest at Parliament Hill blocking all government district access.
protest_ottawa.main_event: Parliament Hill access blocked. Vehicles set on fire on Wellington St.
protest_ottawa.prediction: Government district lockdown required. Secondary protest forming near US Embassy.
protest_ottawa.suggestion: Evacuate non-essential Parliament staff. Activate RCMP emergency protocols.

protest_vancouver.summary: Downtown Vancouver protest turning violent — police overwhelmed, port access threatened.
protest_vancouver.main_event: Vancouver downtown riot. Police requesting backup. Crowd fragmenting toward port.
protest_vancouver.prediction: Port access roads may be blocked within 15 minutes.
protest_vancouver.suggestion: Deploy riot squad. Close Georgia St and Burrard St. Alert Port Authority.

coordinated_pattern.summary: All three cities experiencing simultaneous escalation — pattern inconsistent with organic protest.
coordinated_pattern.main_event: Simultaneous riots in Toronto, Ottawa, and Vancouver confirmed. Coordination suspected.
coordinated_pattern.prediction: Civil unrest may be a precursor to a larger operation. Recommend elevated national alert.
coordinated_pattern.suggestion: Notify CSIS and RCMP intelligence. Begin monitoring for follow-on activity.

predefined_analysis_result.description: Toronto baseline. Morning calm. No anomalies.
Large protest gathering forming at Nathan Phillips Square. Estimated 3,000 people. Crowd agitated, pushing against police lines. Signs in multiple languages.
Simultaneous protest at Parliament Hill Ottawa. Crowd blocking all vehicle access to Wellington St. Police forming defensive line.
Protest march on Georgia St Vancouver heading toward Vancouver Art Gallery. 2,000 participants. Traffic blocked in both directions.
Acoustic sensor Nathan Phillips Square: crowd roar escalating, glass breaking, police sirens. Riot beginning. Multiple injuries reported via 911.
Ottawa — vehicles set on fire on Wellington St. Smoke column visible from Parliament. Police requesting reinforcements. Government district access severed.
Vancouver — crowd turning violent. Store windows smashed on Robson St. Police outnumbered. Transit suspended downtown.
Toronto riot spreading from City Hall toward financial district. Looters visible on Bay St. Police lines broken. Ambulances unable to reach injured.
Ottawa — secondary crowd forming near US Embassy on Sussex Drive. Police resource split across two locations. PM residence security elevated.
Vancouver — riot police deployed. Water cannon activated. Crowd fragmenting into groups moving toward port and Burrard Bridge.
Ham radio operator Toronto: 'Protests in every major city at exactly the same time. This is not spontaneous. This is coordinated. Something is very wrong.'
Toronto financial district — civilians fleeing the riot moving south toward Lake Shore. Dozens of people running in the same direction.
Vancouver — crowd reached port access roads on Burrard Inlet. Port security gates breached. Cargo operations halted.
Vancouver ham radio: 'Protesters are blocking all bridges and the port. They knew exactly where to go. This looks like a deliberate infrastructure shutdown.'
Toronto east of riot zone — streets emptying. Businesses closing early. Transit disrupted. City going quiet.
Ottawa government district locked down. Streets around Parliament empty. Armed RCMP units visible. No resolution in sight.
National ham radio net: operators in Halifax, Winnipeg, and Calgary reporting smaller simultaneous protests in their cities too. Pattern now national in scale.
